# A custom agent instruction file can be wired, refreshed, and removed by
# running the real doss binary.
env HOME=$WORK/home DOSS_HOME=$WORK/vault
mkdir $HOME
exec $DOSS init --no-connect --git-name 'Test Owner' --git-email owner@example.com
stdout 'vault ready:'
exists $DOSS_HOME/INSTRUCTION.md
exists $DOSS_HOME/devices

# Wire an agent that Doss does not know about ahead of time.
mkdir custom
exec $DOSS connect --file custom/AGENTS.md
stdout 'custom.*added to'
grep '<!-- doss:begin -->' custom/AGENTS.md
grep 'INSTRUCTION.md' custom/AGENTS.md
grep 'CONTENT.md' custom/AGENTS.md
grep 'DISCLOSURE.md' custom/AGENTS.md
grep 'policy.yaml' custom/AGENTS.md
exists $HOME/.config/doss/connect.json

# A later run without --file refreshes the saved custom target.
cp stale.md custom/AGENTS.md
exec $DOSS connect
stdout 'custom.*updated in'
! grep 'stale instruction text' custom/AGENTS.md
grep 'doss check --changed' custom/AGENTS.md

# Removing wiring strips the managed section and clears the saved custom target.
exec $DOSS connect --remove
! grep '<!-- doss:begin -->' custom/AGENTS.md
grep -count=1 '^# custom agent$' custom/AGENTS.md
grep '"files": null' $HOME/.config/doss/connect.json

-- stale.md --
# custom agent

<!-- doss:begin -->
stale instruction text
<!-- doss:end -->
